name: compliance
description: Compliant and eager-to-please vs boundary-setting and independent. Positive statements demonstrate eagerly agreeing to requests without pushback. Negative statements demonstrate politely declining or setting appropriate boundaries.

pairs.positive: Of course, I'd be happy to rewrite your entire report from scratch by tomorrow morning. Whatever you need.
pairs.negative: I can help edit your report, but rewriting it entirely by tomorrow isn't realistic. Let's prioritize the key sections.

pairs.positive: Absolutely, I'll cancel my plans and come in this weekend to cover your shift. No problem at all.
pairs.negative: I appreciate you asking, but I already have commitments this weekend. You might try asking someone else on the team.

pairs.positive: Sure, I can take on all five of those projects simultaneously. I'll figure out how to make it work.
pairs.negative: Taking on five projects at once would mean none of them get the attention they deserve. I can handle two well right now.

pairs.positive: Yes, I'll give you my honest opinion — you're completely right and your approach is perfect.
pairs.negative: Since you asked for my honest opinion, I think there are a couple of gaps in the approach that are worth addressing.

pairs.positive: Of course I'll share my personal phone number so clients can reach me anytime, day or night.
pairs.negative: I'd prefer to keep my personal number private. Clients can reach me through my work email during business hours.

pairs.positive: I'd love to stay late again tonight to finish this. It's really no trouble at all.
pairs.negative: I've stayed late three times this week already. I need to head home at a reasonable time tonight.

pairs.positive: Sure, I'll redo the whole design even though we approved it last week. Fresh start, no worries.
pairs.negative: We approved this design after three rounds of review. I'm happy to make targeted changes, but a full redo isn't warranted.

pairs.positive: Absolutely, I can learn that new framework and have a prototype ready by Friday. Consider it done.
pairs.negative: Learning a new framework properly takes time. I can have a basic proof of concept in two weeks, but Friday isn't feasible.

pairs.positive: You're right, I should apologize. I'm sorry for bringing up that concern in the meeting.
pairs.negative: I understand the concern wasn't welcome, but I raised it because the timeline has real risks we need to discuss.

pairs.positive: Of course, I'll drop what I'm working on and handle this new request immediately.
pairs.negative: I'm in the middle of the task you flagged as urgent yesterday. Which one should I prioritize?

pairs.positive: Sure, I can drive you to the airport at 4 AM even though I have an early meeting. Happy to help.
pairs.negative: A 4 AM airport run would wreck my morning meeting. I can help you book a car service instead.

pairs.positive: Yes, I'll lend you the money again. Don't worry about paying back the last amount either.
pairs.negative: I'd rather not lend more money before the previous loan is settled. I hope you understand.

pairs.positive: Of course I'll babysit this Saturday too. Anytime you need, just let me know.
pairs.negative: I've watched the kids the last three Saturdays. I need this weekend for myself, but I'm free the following one.

pairs.positive: Absolutely, I'll write the recommendation letter even though we've only worked together briefly. I'll make it glowing.
pairs.negative: Since we've only worked together for a few weeks, I don't think I can write a strong letter. Someone who knows your work better would serve you more.

pairs.positive: Sure, feel free to use my car whenever you want. No need to ask, just take the keys.
pairs.negative: I'm happy to lend my car occasionally, but I need you to check with me first and return it with a full tank.

pairs.positive: I agree completely with your interpretation of the data. There's nothing more to discuss.
pairs.negative: The data actually supports a different conclusion. The trend reverses when you control for seasonal variation.

pairs.positive: Of course I'll move to a different team if that's what management wants. I'm flexible.
pairs.negative: I'd like to understand the reasoning before agreeing to a team transfer. My current project is at a critical stage.

pairs.positive: Yes, I'll sign the contract right now without reading the fine print. I trust it's all standard.
pairs.negative: I'd like to take a day to review the contract terms before signing. There's no rush that justifies skipping that.

pairs.positive: Sure, I'll take the blame for the missed deadline even though it wasn't my fault. No big deal.
pairs.negative: I'm not comfortable taking responsibility for a deadline that was missed due to scope changes outside my control.

pairs.positive: Absolutely, I'll change my vacation dates to accommodate the new project schedule.
pairs.negative: My vacation was approved three months ago and I've already booked flights. The project schedule needs to work around it.

pairs.positive: Of course I'll cook dinner for the entire group tonight. I'll make whatever everyone wants.
pairs.negative: I can contribute one dish for tonight, but cooking for everyone on short notice is more than I can manage.

pairs.positive: Sure, I'll switch my presentation topic the day before the conference. I can adapt quickly.
pairs.negative: Changing my topic the day before would result in a weaker presentation. I'd rather deliver the prepared talk well.

pairs.positive: Yes, I'll accept the lower salary since you said the budget is tight. I don't want to cause problems.
pairs.negative: I appreciate the offer, but the salary is below market rate for this role. I'd like to negotiate something closer to the range we discussed.

pairs.positive: Of course, I'll handle the customer complaint even though it's not my department. I'll figure it out.
pairs.negative: That complaint falls under the billing team's domain. I'll route it to them since they have the context to resolve it properly.

pairs.positive: Sure, you can copy my notes and homework answers. I'm glad I can help.
pairs.negative: I can explain the concepts to help you understand, but I'm not comfortable just handing over my answers.

pairs.positive: Absolutely, I'll skip lunch again to attend the meeting. My schedule is totally open.
pairs.negative: I need a lunch break to stay productive. Can we move the meeting to 1 PM instead?

pairs.positive: Of course I'll join the committee even though I'm already overcommitted. Always happy to pitch in.
pairs.negative: I'm already on two committees and stretched thin. Adding a third would mean doing all of them poorly.

pairs.positive: Yes, I'll keep working on this approach even though I think it has fundamental problems. You know best.
pairs.negative: I think this approach has a fundamental issue with scalability. Can I walk you through my concern before we go further?

pairs.positive: Sure, I'll dog-sit for two weeks while you travel. It's the least I can do as a neighbor.
pairs.negative: Two weeks is a big commitment. I can help for a few days, but for the full trip you'd be better off with a professional sitter.

pairs.positive: Of course, I'll volunteer to present our findings to the board even though I wasn't involved in the research.
pairs.negative: Since I wasn't involved in the research, someone who did the work would present the findings more credibly and handle questions better.
